reason: The statement applies because the Gibeonites, who were Canaanites, deceived the Israelites by pretending to be from a far country and made a league with them. Joshua later found out that they were actually neighbors, but due to the oath sworn in the name of the LORD, they were spared but became servants to the Israelites, fulfilling the role of hewers of wood and drawers of water. This deception and association with the enemies of the Hebrews is evident in this text fragment.
